# reading-flow/order

<FeatureLinks mdn="https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/search?q=reading-flow%2Forder%20CSS" caniuse="https://caniuse.com/?search=reading-flow" status="Display 4" label="Reference links" />

## Plain-language explanation

Control accessible reading and tab order when visual layout is rearranged.

## Representative syntax

```css
reading-flow: flex-visual; reading-order: 1;
```

## Demo

<CssDemo title="reading-flow/order" css-b64="" code-b64="" html-b64="" caption="The form demo keeps native controls and shows how CSS can shape the interaction surface without losing semantics." lang="en-US" badge="Practical demo" />

## When to use it

<div class="usage-grid">
<section>
  <h3>Best fit</h3>
  <p>Use reading-flow/order in complex layout when you need to express alignment, wrapping, and order as layout rules instead of extra wrapper markup. It usually gives the stylesheet a clearer job than extra classes, wrappers, or script.</p>
</section>
<section>
  <h3>Example</h3>
  <p>A settings page can align every nested control row while a responsive gallery chooses the right packing behavior.</p>
</section>
<section>
  <h3>Watch out</h3>
  <p>Layout features can change reading and focus order indirectly. Check the experience with keyboard navigation, not only screenshots.</p>
</section>
</div>

## Source metadata

- Category: Forms, Controls, and Interactive UI
- Maturity / source: Display 4
- Representative syntax: `reading-flow: flex-visual; reading-order: 1;`
- Position in category: 12 / 12
